topic: Accuracy-math demotion (Phase A2): hero sublabel arithmetic clause + bordered accent box consolidated into a single <aside class=accuracy-math-aside>
rubric_applied: operator truth-vs-standards rubric (design-category EXP register): full adversarial weight on on-page truth impact, relaxed register on proposal-prose pedantry
our_argument_summary: EXP-211-redesign proposes four coordinated edits to build-scripts/generate-html.js: (1) strip the Honest-accuracy arithmetic clause out of the hero Actual-Failures sc-card sublabel, keeping the He-admits/We-found narrative and the #p3-failures Details link; (2) delete the bordered accent box (border:2px solid var(--accent)) at the bottom of the Overview that carries the headline-is-hardcoded critique; (3) insert a new <aside class="accuracy-math-aside"> after the sc-breakdown block and before the Downloads heading consolidating the moved arithmetic and the full source-code critique in a two-column grid at >=720px, stacked below; (4) add .accuracy-math-aside CSS (0.92rem body in --ink-2, 3px border-left in --rule, transparent bg, kicker in --ink-3 small-caps sans). No changes to data/sections.json. All arithmetic remains computed from generate-html.js context variables.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Not applicable — EXP target is our review build pipeline, not dome code.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Every number in the proposed aside is templated from existing context vars: counts.total, failures.dome_claimed_failures, silentFailures, honestAccuracy, failures.dome_claimed_accuracy, accuracyVariantList. NC5 (computed-counts rule) honored.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: None introduced by this EXP. Ordering dependency on EXP-202 (tokens) documented in integration_hold.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: Minor documentation inconsistency: EXP-211-redesign acceptance_criteria item 12 asserts EXP-202 dark --ink-2 = #c7c9d9, but the actual EXP-202 value is #b8b5ad. Both values pass WCAG AA on #1a1a2e (10.38:1 vs 8.33:1). Documentation error only; no functional impact. Relax register per rubric (proposal-prose inconsistency without on-page render impact).

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Not applicable — design-category EXP, not a WIN.

holes_found.description: The bordered-accent-box closer sentence "The headline number is manually entered with a self-serving denominator." is dropped by this EXP. The analyst frames this as "deliberate tradeoff" arguing factual substrate carries the claim under the readers own power. Under the operators truth-vs-standards rubric this is TRUTH-IMPACTING ("any rewrite that softens, hedges, or dilutes the critique ... relative to the current on-site version"). "Self-serving" is the editorial verdict that makes the critique sharp — its the single sentence where the review stops describing and starts naming the pattern. The preserved clauses describe what the denominator excludes; only this sentence names the intent. Restraint-by-default is the operators stated direction, but the rubric explicitly orders strong escalation on critique softening, which outranks restraint when the two conflict.
holes_found.recommendation: Re-insert the sentence in the ama-col-source paragraph, either verbatim or in the slightly-softened form "The headline number is manually entered, and the denominator excludes categories the dome has no interest in acknowledging." If the human group affirms the softening tradeoff after seeing this flag, document that decision explicitly in the EXPs notes_for_human_review_group and close the finding. Otherwise restore. Two-word re-insertion is the minimum viable fix.

holes_found.description: The ama-variant and ama-cross paragraphs render at 0.88rem in --ink-3. On the dark theme, --ink-3 (#8a8680) on page bg (#1a1a2e) measures 4.71:1 — passes WCAG AA 4.5:1 normal-text threshold, but with a margin of 0.21. Acceptance_criteria item 12 only tests --ink-2 (8.33:1, comfortable). If a future token tweak (e.g., a Phase B palette adjustment on --ink-3 or --bg) pushes ink-3 contrast below 4.5:1, the ama-variant paragraph — which carries "Internal queries return {list} depending on which data source and counting method is used" and the "denominator is chosen to include only N acknowledged falsifications while excluding unresolved open problems" substrate — will be rendering truth-content at sub-AA contrast. That substrate is the factual load-bearing content behind the (dropped) "self-serving" claim. Same concern applies to ama-cross (source-code critique cross-ref to #timestamp-error). This is an a11y functional concern per the rubric, and the current thin margin deserves a regression guard, not just a one-shot check.
holes_found.recommendation: Add an acceptance_criterion that explicitly measures --ink-3 on page bg at 0.88rem and asserts >=4.5:1 with a documented safety margin (recommend >=5.0:1 to give a small regression buffer). Include the measurement in the test.js color-pair contrast assertion alongside the existing --ink-2 check. If the contrast budget is too tight, either (a) swap ama-variant/ama-cross to --ink-2 (sacrificing some visual hierarchy but gaining contrast headroom), or (b) nudge --ink-3 dark value lighter in a coordinated EXP-202 amendment.

holes_found.category: truth-impacting: screen-reader context for source-code critique
holes_found.description: The proposed semantic element is <aside>. Under WAI-ARIA, <aside> is announced as "complementary" by most screen readers, and users commonly have a "skip complementary landmarks" navigation preference. For a reader scanning by landmark, the source-code-hardcoded critique ("not computed by any script", ".score-number CSS class", "No Python script, no JavaScript function, and no API endpoint", "no script validates the count") — which IS load-bearing critique content, not tangential commentary — may be bypassed entirely. The analyst explicitly acknowledges in anticipated_objections #7 that a strict WAI-ARIA reading favors <section aria-labelledby> for directly related supporting detail, and defers the decision to EXP-A8 accessibility audit. Per the rubric, "a11y functional concerns ... screen-reader context loss" is truth-impacting, which argues against deferral when the element choice is already known to be borderline.
holes_found.recommendation: Change the element to <section aria-labelledby="accuracy-math-aside-heading"> and promote .ama-kicker from <div> to a styled <h3> (or <div role="heading" aria-level="3">) to give the section an accessible heading. Keep the kicker styled as a small-caps eyebrow — the existing TOC generator (which picks up h1/h2/h3) can be patched to skip headings inside elements with a data-toc-exclude attribute, OR the kicker can stay a div with role=heading aria-level=3 which most screen readers expose without the TOC regex catching it. If the human group prefers deferring to EXP-A8, document the explicit trade (screen-reader users may skip the source-code critique until A8 lands) in notes_for_human_review_group and set a hard deadline on A8.

holes_found.description: CC2 in the source brief requires one-scroll reachability from the hero to the accuracy-math content. In the current layout (before this EXP), the bordered accent box sits immediately after the sc-breakdown block; a skim-reader at 1080p sees it within one viewport scroll from the hero because the breaking-news block + sc-breakdown fit above the fold boundary. Post-EXP, the aside sits in the same slot but at reduced pitch — and the EXPs acceptance_criterion (item 17) relaxes CC2 to "if the bar chart pushes the aside out of the first scroll, the aside first arithmetic line must still be reachable within two scrolls." Combined with the visual demotion (no border, no background, muted ink, kicker signaling "skip this"), a reader who does not scroll twice and does not perceive the aside as primary content may leave the Overview without the hardcoded-number critique. That is a truth-surface-area loss specifically on the critique the hardcoded-dome-count + Timestamp-Error cross-reference depends on.
holes_found.recommendation: Keep the demotion pitch — thats endorsed by the brief — but either (a) tighten CC2 back to one-scroll-target by moving the aside earlier (e.g., between sc-breakdown and Latest Findings, or immediately after Latest Findings), or (b) add a one-line hero sc-sublabel back-reference like "See the full accuracy math below" inside the Actual-Failures card (5 words, does not restore the arithmetic to the hero, but restores the signal that supporting detail exists). Measure actual scroll distance at 1080p AND 1440p AND common mobile (iPhone 15 Pro 393x852) via Playwright and put the measured numbers in the acceptance_criterion, not just a boolean.

holes_found.description: Kicker text "Accuracy math — detail for the calibrated reader" editorializes about the audience. "Calibrated reader" signals skippability, which aligns with the demotion intent, but also reads slightly condescending on a review that otherwise avoids audience-framing tropes. The analyst already flagged this for human review with alternatives; not a truth concern on its own.
holes_found.recommendation: Pick "Accuracy math — supporting detail" (analysts second-choice default) unless the operator specifically wants the skippability signal. Relax register on this finding per rubric (proposal-prose pedantry).

holes_found.category: relax-register: defensive rendering acceptance criterion absent
holes_found.description: Anticipated_objections #6 identifies a grammar failure when accuracyVariantList is empty ("Internal queries return  depending on which..." produces a double-space), and the EXP proposes a ternary wrap. The ternary wrap is described in the objection response as an "IMPLEMENTATION NOTE" rather than added to change_3 new_aside_html or to acceptance_criteria. This is a test-harness robustness concern without current-DOM failure (the variant list is currently non-empty), and the rubric places it in the relax-register bucket. However, leaving the implementation instruction only in the objection response means the decider could miss it during integration.
holes_found.recommendation:
Hoist the ternary wrap into change_3 new_aside_html literal text, and add an explicit acceptance_criterion (d) "when variantSources is empty, docs/index.html contains no

element and no double-space sequence within the aside." Preferred because it removes a single-integrator-memory dependency.

holes_found.description: Two-column grid at >=720px gives tablet portrait (768-1024px) ~340px per column, which fits ~50 characters per line at 0.92rem. Tight but functional. The EXP proposes a two-breakpoint scheme (stack <=720px, two-column >720px); a three-breakpoint scheme (stack <=720, single-column 720-960, two-column >=960) would be gentler on tablet portrait at the cost of one extra media query.
holes_found.recommendation: Defer to human group preference. Relax register per rubric.

holes_found.category: relax-register: documentation drift in token reference
holes_found.description: acceptance_criteria item 12 cites "EXP-202: dark = #c7c9d9" for --ink-2. The actual EXP-202 dark --ink-2 value is #b8b5ad. Both pass AA. Documentation drift only.
holes_found.recommendation: Update the citation to #b8b5ad when the EXP is written up; no functional impact. Relax register.

stronger_arguments.description: The aside introduces an anchor to #timestamp-error that was only implicit in the deleted bordered box. Making this cross-reference explicit is a genuine gain for the review — the Timestamp Error tab leans on the same hardcoded-number pattern (status_history.json parameters baked into HTML rather than derived at build time) that the bordered box called out for the accuracy percentage. The EXP under-sells this: the cross-reference is not just preservation-plus, it strengthens the rhetorical structure of the overall review by tying Part 6 (source-code analysis) and the Timestamp Error tab together. If the curmudgeons other findings here are addressed, the reviewer should consider adding symmetric back-references from the Timestamp Error tab to the accuracy-math aside, closing the loop.

The aside newly writes out the honest-accuracy denominator formula explicitly: counts.total / (counts.total + failures.dome_claimed_failures + silentFailures). The current hero sublabel only names the result ("Honest accuracy: X, not Y") without showing the math. Moving to a lower-pitch surface that shows the full formula is arguably a net gain in auditability — a calibrated reader can check our arithmetic from the aside; a skim-reader at hero pitch couldnt. This is under-credited in the EXPs kernel_of_truth.

kernel_of_truth.description: The EXP genuinely delivers on the brief. Six of seven source-code-critique clauses from the bordered accent box are preserved verbatim. All arithmetic remains live-templated (no new hardcoded numbers anywhere in the aside). Every existing internal anchor (#p3-failures, #part6) survives; #timestamp-error is added as a new cross-reference that strengthens the thematic link between the hardcoded-accuracy critique and the Timestamp Error tab. The denominator formula is written out explicitly for the first time. The aria-labelledby on the aside points to the kicker id, giving the element an accessible name. Seven NCs (NC1 dark theme through NC7 tests-green) are each mapped to explicit preservation mechanisms. The demotion pitch (0.92rem body, 3px rule border, no card bg, kicker signalling skippability) is visibly lower than the deleted bordered accent box (1rem body, 2px accent border, card bg, pull-quote-weight prose) — the EXP achieves the brief s visual goal without gutting the content.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Not applicable — this is a design EXP with no scientific claim to save. The critique applies to the redraft s execution, not its intent. The intent (demote pitch, preserve critique) is correct; the execution has one material critique-softening gap (drop of "self-serving denominator"), two moderate a11y concerns (thin --ink-3 contrast budget without regression guard, <aside> vs <section> ambiguity), and one moderate CC2 weakening (one-scroll becoming effectively two-scroll). None of these invalidate the EXP; all are addressable with minor edits before integration.

blocking_for_integration: Restore "self-serving denominator" sentence OR obtain explicit human-group affirmation that the softening is intentional; currently the softening is described as a tradeoff without group sign-off.
Add a --ink-3 contrast acceptance criterion alongside the existing --ink-2 one; the 4.71:1 margin is thin and the ama-variant/ama-cross paragraphs render critique substrate.
non_blocking_but_recommended: Reconsider <aside> vs <section aria-labelledby> — if deferring to EXP-A8 is the group s preference, document the explicit screen-reader skip trade in notes_for_human_review_group with an A8 deadline.
Tighten CC2 acceptance from "within two scrolls" to "measured scroll distance at 1080p/1440p/mobile, with targets"; consider moving the aside one slot earlier to restore one-scroll reachability.
Hoist the ama-variant ternary wrap into change_3 new_aside_html literal text and add an explicit empty-variantSources acceptance criterion.
Pick "Accuracy math — supporting detail" for the kicker unless skippability signaling is explicitly wanted.
Fix the #c7c9d9 vs #b8b5ad documentation drift in acceptance_criteria item 12.

deeper_analysis: This review applies the operator s truth-vs-standards rubric (per HNOTE-CURMUDGEON-EXP-211-REVIEW-RUBRIC-001) rather than the default dome-content adversarial register. The rubric keeps full adversarial weight on findings affecting how truth claims render on the page and relaxes register on proposal-prose pedantry. Three findings meet the truth-impacting bar (critique softening via dropped sentence; thin --ink-3 contrast budget for critique-substrate paragraphs; <aside> semantic element potentially hiding critique from screen-reader landmark-skip users), one is truth-impacting via visual reachability (CC2 weakening), and four are relax-register (kicker phrasing, defensive-rendering acceptance criterion absence, tablet breakpoint tightness, token reference drift). The largest single concern is the dropped "self-serving denominator" sentence — it is the only sentence in the deleted bordered box that names the pattern rather than describing it, and the operator s rubric explicitly orders strong escalation on critique softening relative to the current on-site version. The analyst s defense (reader reaches the conclusion under their own power) is a legitimate rhetorical position but is in tension with the rubric s plain language. Second-largest concern is the <aside> element choice — the analyst flagged the WAI-ARIA mismatch themselves and deferred to EXP-A8, but the rubric treats screen-reader context loss as truth-impacting which argues against deferral. Third is the --ink-3 contrast budget — current numbers pass AA but with only 0.21 margin over threshold; the acceptance_criteria explicitly tests only --ink-2; a future palette adjustment could silently push ink-3 below AA and ink-3 carries critique substrate. CC2 weakening is real but less severe because the arithmetic remains on the Overview, just further down. Everything else falls into the relax-register bucket per the rubric and should not block integration. Verdict: recommended_action is minor_edit — the EXP is close to shippable, needs two blocking edits (restore self-serving or confirm softening; add ink-3 contrast test) before operator approval. Other side of the argument the user asked me to consider: the operator s restraint-by-default direction is explicit and the analyst s defense of the "self-serving" drop is aligned with it; if the human group reads the rubric with restraint as the default and truth-impact as the exception, the drop could reasonably stand and the minor_edit shrinks to just the ink-3 contrast test. That judgment belongs to the group, not the curmudgeon.

citations_verified: Hero sc-sublabel current text matches EXP description (verified line 1258 of build-scripts/generate-html.js)
Bordered accent box current HTML matches EXP description (verified lines 1318-1320)
citations_failed: EXP-211-redesign acceptance_criteria item 12 cites EXP-202 dark --ink-2 = #c7c9d9; actual value is #b8b5ad. Both pass AA but documentation drift should be fixed.
citations_unchecked: HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A-001 (not read in this review session)

other_side_of_argument: Per the user preference to consider the counter-case: if the human group treats "restraint-by-default" as the operative principle and "self-serving denominator" as falling inside editorial-chrome rather than factual-critique content, then the drop of that sentence is a correct execution of the brief, not a truth-softening. The factual substrate ("denominator excludes unresolved open problems and below-detection-threshold entries") survives and supports the same conclusion under the readers own inference. Under that reading, the only real blocker is the --ink-3 contrast regression guard, and the EXP is essentially shippable with a two-line test addition. The curmudgeons major-severity escalation on the dropped sentence is defensible under the rubrics strict text but softer under its spirit if restraint outweighs named-pattern force. The human group should make this call explicitly; the curmudgeon flags it rather than resolves it.
